summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Upstox-style mock trading app. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passed (auth, market, orders, portfolio). Frontend: splash->login->OAuth simulation->dashboard flow works; 4 indices and 10 watchlist stocks render; token persists in localStorage; auto-login on reload skips to dashboard; order modal opens. One minor UI issue: Auto SL/Target switches sit below mobile viewport (390x844) inside OrderModal and require scrolling.

ui_bugs.issue: Auto SL switch and Auto Target switch are below the 390x844 viewport fold; modal sheet content not scrollable causing Playwright 'Element outside viewport' error when interacting. Real users on small phones may also need to scroll but modal lacks ScrollView.

issues: Bottom sheet is tall; Place Order button risks being below fold on small devices. Wrap sheet content in ScrollView or reduce vertical paddings.

action_items: Wrap OrderModal sheet content inside a ScrollView so Auto SL switch, Auto Target switch, and Place Order button are reachable on small (<=400pt height) viewports.
(Optional) expo-secure-store version 55.0.13 vs expected ~15.0.8 warning in expo logs — verify on native build.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: place_order returns price 0 if order_type=='MARKET' lookup misses; current code falls back to random — OK for mock.
Backend correctly excludes Mongo _id via projection in list_orders.
Frontend api.ts has proper Bearer auth header injection and localStorage fallback for web.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (core flows pass; modal scroll minor issue)

seed_data_creation: Tests create transient orders in MongoDB orders collection (prefixed with ORD ids), then cancel them. No cleanup required for mock data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Mock OAuth: POST /api/auth/upstox/callback with any non-empty code returns upx_mock_<hex> token. Bearer required for /auth/me, /orders/*, /portfolio. Frontend testIDs used: splash-screen, login-with-upstox-button, login-step-label, dashboard-scroll, index-*, stock-*, buy-*, sell-*, order-modal, qty-plus/minus/input, auto-sl-switch, auto-target-switch, place-order-button, logout-button.
rca_of_issue: OrderModal renders content vertically without an internal ScrollView; on a 390x844 phone viewport the Auto SL/Target rows + Place Order button overflow below screen edge. Repro: open dashboard, tap any B/S — Switches are not initially visible. Mitigation: wrap modal sheet body in <ScrollView> or set maxHeight + scrollEnabled.
